name: Olga Samaroff
abstract: Olga Samaroff (born Lucy Mary Agnes Hickenlooper; August 8, 1880 – May 17, 1948) was an American pianist, music critic, and teacher. Among her teachers was Charles-Valentin Alkan's son, Élie-Miriam Delaborde.

about_quotes.speaker: John Budd
about_quotes.evidence: And Olga Samaroff was a Polish girl who played a marvelous tune called “Sparkputz.” Other musicians tell me it’s a great favorite, a difficult tune. But I learned about those people
about_quotes.doc_title: John Budd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [transcript only]
